sections.paragraph:
ἄωρος, ον (s. ὥρα; Aeschyl., Hdt.+; PSI 1057, 11 and 17; PBon 19, 10 and 15; PGM 4, 2877; 5, 332; LXX; TestAbr A 18 p. 100, 25 [Stone p. 48] ἀώρως; 19 p. 102, 22f v.l.-ος; Jos., Bell. 4, 502) untimely=too early (a term found esp. in grave ins RLattimore, Themes in Gk. and Lat. Epitaphs ’62, 185f; ZNW 22, 1923, 281 nos. 5, 3; 6, 5; 7, 2 al.; JKazazis, Hellenika 40, ’89, 21–33; s. also Preis. ind.) of children οἵτινες ἄ. ἐτίκτοντο who were born prematurely ApcPt 11, 26.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

PBon PBon = Papyri Bononienses—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

esp. esp. = especially

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Gk. Gk. = Greek

Lat. Lat. = Latin

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Preis. Preis. = FPreisigke, Wörterbuch der griech. Papyrusurkunden—Lists 4, 6

ind. ind. = index

ApcPt ApcPt = Apocalypse of Peter—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 161
